pr.title: Match remaining Ness items except yoyo
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: Large Ness item matching PR that filled in remaining Ness item implementations except yoyo, primarily across PK Thunder ball/trail, PK Flash/Flush, PK Fire, PK Flash explosion, and Ness bat item files. It also updated item variable/header types and made a small Ness SpecialHi type/stack-layout adjustment. Bot report recorded +9524 matched code bytes, +200 matched data bytes, and 37 new matches.

key_files.role: largest implementation target; bot report lists multiple new 100% matches including it_802AB58C, itNesspkthunderball_UnkMotion0_Phys, it_802AC098, it_802ABA4C, itNesspkthunderball_UnkMotion0_Anim, it_802AB90C, and it_802AB9C0
PK Flash/Flush implementation target; adds spawn, destroyed callback, motion anim/phys/coll behavior, scaling helper, owner cleanup, Ness/Kirby owner-specific switch handling
Ness bat implementation target; adds spawn functions, owner-reference cleanup helpers, animation logic, JObj flag/scale behavior, and typed owner pointer usage
PK Thunder trail implementation target; bot report lists new 100% matches and .sdata2 progress
PK Flash explosion implementation target; bot report lists new 100% matches including itNesspkflushexplode_UnkMotion0_Anim, it_802AFA70, and it_802AF940
PK Fire implementation target; adds spawn function and shield-bounce rotation inversion logic
header/type cleanup; collapses repeated Vec3 and float fields into positions[16] and angles[16], and changes several later fields from HSD_GObj* to s32/f32 where shown
item variable typing; changes Ness bat item var x0 from s32 to HSD_GObj* and adds itNesspkthundertrail_ItemVars
item variable type correction; changes itPKFlushExplode_ItemVars.xDD8 from s32 to float
small fighter-side adjustment for PK Thunder Ness collision helper; check_distance and locals changed from Vec2 to Vec3 with stack padding changes
prototype cleanup; it_802AD478 signature changes s32/float to Fighter_Part/f32
header changed for PK Flush implementation; exact diff not loaded
header changed for PK Flush explosion implementation; exact diff not loaded
header changed for PK Thunder ball implementation; exact diff not loaded
header changed for PK Thunder trail implementation; exact diff not loaded
shared item type header changed; exact diff not loaded

what_changed: Replaced many placeholder comments in Ness item source files with matched C implementations for PK Thunder ball/trail, PK Flash/Flush, PK Flash explosion, PK Fire, and Ness bat.
Updated item-variable structs to use more accurate typed fields, including HSD_GObj*/Item_GObj* ownership references, float fields, and PK Thunder positions/angles arrays.
Added or adjusted item spawn setup patterns using SpawnItem, Item_80268B18, parent gobj assignment, facing_dir, zeroed velocity, life timers, and item command vars.
Added owner-reference cleanup for Ness bat and PK Flash/Flush paths, including Ness/Kirby-specific callbacks when item ownership is still valid.
Used baselib JObj helpers for child lookup, flags, scale, rotation get/set, and copied scale behavior.
Adjusted ftNs_SpecialHi collision helper locals from Vec2 to Vec3 and changed padding sizes around those locals.
Updated headers/prototypes to match recovered function signatures and item variable layouts.
Bot report recorded 37 new matches, +0.25% matched code, +9524 matched code bytes, +0.02% matched data, and +200 matched data bytes.

smart_moves.text: Grouped repeated PK Thunder history fields into arrays (`positions[16]`, `angles[16]`) instead of retaining many individually named offset fields.
Recovered item variable types before using them heavily in item logic, e.g. Ness bat owner field as `HSD_GObj*`, PK Thunder trail item vars, and PK Flash explosion float field.
Factored small local inline helpers for repeated source-level operations such as PK Flash scale computation and Ness bat owner cleanup/scale copy.
Kept cross-system ownership behavior explicit by switching between Ness and Kirby PK Flash/Flush callbacks based on item kind.
Updated public prototypes when function signatures became clearer, rather than leaving old `s32`/`float` forms in headers.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When item state shows a run of same-sized history fields, an array field can be a better source hypothesis than many offset-named scalar fields; in this PR, PK Thunder changed many Vec3/float fields into `positions[16]` and `angles[16]`.

Recovering typed item-var fields can unlock readable item implementations: this PR typed owner references and float fields, then used those fields directly in Ness bat, PK Flash/Flush, and explosion logic.
For character-owned articles, matched item code may need to preserve owner back-reference cleanup and fighter-specific callbacks, including copy-character variants such as Kirby.
Header signature cleanup should accompany recovered function bodies when parameter roles become clear, as shown by `it_802AD478` changing from `s32, float` to `Fighter_Part, f32` in the header.

naming_conventions.name_or_pattern: PK Thunder history arrays named `positions` and `angles`
naming_conventions.context: Replaces many offset-style fields in `itPKThunder_ItemVars` where the diff supports repeated Vec3 and float arrays.

naming_conventions.name_or_pattern: `owner`/owner-reference naming for item vars where a fighter/object back-reference is used
naming_conventions.context: `xDE0_PKFlash_Owner` is used as the PK Flash/Flush owner back-reference and checked against `ip->owner`; Ness bat still uses `x0` but is typed as `HSD_GObj*` and assigned owner gobjs.
The new item implementations use conventional compact locals for Item pointers, JObj pointers, SpawnItem, special attributes, spawned items, and owner references.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was used narrowly in several matched item functions and the SpecialHi adjustment changed padding around Vec3 locals.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The PR evidence includes bot match results, but no detailed objdiff notes explaining each PAD_STACK instance; keep as PR-local tactic evidence, not a global style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used static inline helpers for repeated operations while still matching many functions, including PK Flash scale computation and Ness bat owner/scale helpers.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Evidence shows these helpers in a merged PR with reported matches, but does not prove the helpers were required for matching.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Matched JObj operations through existing helper calls such as `HSD_JObjGetChild`, `HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll`, `HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll`, `HSD_JObjGetScaleY`, `HSD_JObjSetScale`, `HSD_JObjGetRotationZ`, and `HSD_JObjSetRotationZ`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: No review discussion confirms why these exact helper forms were chosen.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used typed structs and arrays to replace offset-heavy/generated-looking state access in headers before implementing functions against those fields.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Only visible diff and bot report are available; full function-level verification details are not loaded.

Search for later cleanup of `PAD_STACK` in Ness item functions from PR 2284.
Search `itNesspkflush_SetScale` and `copy_jobj_scale` for follow-up refactors or reviewer changes.
Search later PRs for `xDE0_PKFlash_Owner` naming or stronger semantic names for PK Flash item vars.

Search for `fighter-decomp Skill` or `Claude` in later PR bodies to track tool-assisted decomp workflow evolution.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Array fields can replace repeated item-var offset fields when the diff supports repeated history/state slots
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2284 changed PK Thunder item vars from many repeated Vec3 and float offset fields into `positions[16]` and `angles[16]`, supporting a PR-local lesson to look for arrays in repeated item state.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed ItemVars made Ness item implementations more readable and matchable in this PR
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2284 corrected or added typed item-var fields, including Ness bat owner pointer, PK Thunder trail vars, PK Flash explosion float field, and PK Thunder arrays, then used those fields in matched item implementations.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Narrow PAD_STACK use appears in matched Ness item functions
accepted_candidate_records.text: Several newly matched functions in PR #2284 include small `PAD_STACK` uses, and the bot report records new 100% matches. The slice does not include enough evidence to generalize this beyond PR-local matching tactics.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider documenting repeated item-var history fields as array candidates
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2284 provides evidence that repeated offset fields in item vars may become arrays when the source likely stored history/state slots, as with PK Thunder `positions[16]` and `angles[16]`. This may fit the existing natural-loops/typed-fields standards but could be curated as an item-var-specific example rather than a new rule.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful concrete example of replacing repeated generated-looking offset fields with typed arrays, but one PR is not enough to establish a broad standard.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider adding a cautionary example for PAD_STACK in matched item functions
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2284 used several narrow `PAD_STACK` instances in functions reported as newly matched. This supports adding an example under the existing matching-tactics standard, emphasizing that PAD_STACK should remain narrow and tied to match evidence.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR has match report evidence but not detailed objdiff rationale for each PAD_STACK, so curator should phrase this as an example/caution rather than endorsement.

source_update_candidates.title: Ness item implementations span item files plus fighter owner callbacks
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2284 shows Ness item matching in `src/melee/it/items/itness*.c` may require fighter headers/callbacks from Ness and Kirby copy-special code, e.g. `ftNs_SpecialN`, `ftKb_Init`, and `ftNs_AttackS4` owner cleanup paths.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially useful source/path fact, but should be curated against current tree before adding.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR body's Claude/harness comments into a decomp standard; it is workflow context, not evidence for a code style rule.

Do not infer that all Ness item functions except yoyo are currently matched from this slice alone; the title and bot report support many matches, but the loaded diff is truncated and historical state may have changed.
Do not generalize `PAD_STACK` as preferred style; the loaded evidence only supports that it appeared in this PR alongside reported matches.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale; no human review comments were loaded.
